summary: Split the game-mode translation unit formerly embedded in gm_16AE.c at address 0x8016F180 into new files gm_16F1.c and gm_16F1.static.h, with matching split metadata and build configuration updates. The move transferred gm_16F1 text/data/bss/sdata2 ownership, cleaned gm_16AE.static.h down to only the remaining gm_16AE-owned declarations/data, and preserved/re-established matches for 14 functions in the new gm_16F1 unit while gm_80173EEC became an 84.24% unmatched function in that new unit.
what_changed: Added a new build object, Object(NonMatching, "melee/gm/gm_16F1.c"), in configure.py.
Updated config/GALE01/splits.txt so gm_16AE.c ends at text address 0x8016F180 and new gm_16F1.c owns text 0x8016F180-0x80174274.

Moved gm_16F1-related .data, .bss, .sbss, .sdata2, and rodata ranges out of the old gm_16AE split range: gm_16AE now owns data 0x803D5620-0x803D5648, bss 0x8046B6A0-0x8046DBC8, sdata2 0x804DA298-0x804DA2E8; gm_16F1 owns data 0x803D5648-0x803D6488, bss 0x8046DBC8-0x8046DBE8, sbss 0x804D65A0-0x804D65B8, sdata2 0x804DA2E8-0x804DA300, and rodata 0x803B7A60-0x803B7B18.

Created src/melee/gm/gm_16F1.c containing functions previously at the tail of gm_16AE.c, including fn_8016F180, gmDecisionGetType, fn_8016F1F0, gm_8016F208, fn_8016F280, gm_8016F2F8, fn_8016F344, fn_80171AD4, gm_801701A0, fn_801701AC, fn_801701B8, fn_801724C8, gm_801736DC, gm_801737D8, and gm_80173EEC.
Created src/melee/gm/gm_16F1.static.h for gm_16F1-owned statics, including lbl_8046DBC8, lbl_8046DBD8, lbl_803D5648, lbl_803D5A4C, and lbl_804D65A0.
Reduced src/melee/gm/gm_16AE.static.h from a large mixed static header to a much smaller gm_16AE-local header, removing gm_16F1-owned structs, arrays, and prototypes.
Moved some formerly static declarations into the public gm_1601.h interface: fn_80160840(u8), gm_801736DC(void), and changed gm_80160854's return type from u8 to int.
Removed static declarations for fn_80160840 and gm_801736DC from gm_1601.static.h.

Stopped gmresult.c and gmresultplayer.c from including gm_16AE.static.h; gmresult.c now declares its local lbl_8046DBE8_t and static lbl_8046DBE8, while gmresultplayer.c declares static int lbl_8046E38C[4].

In remaining gm_16AE.c, removed the lblanguage include and introduced small helper functions get_unk_float and direction, with minor float literal capitalization and PAD_STACK adjustment in nearby functions.
smart_moves: The split was done at both source and binary-section levels: configure.py added the new object, and splits.txt carved the original gm_16AE ranges at exact text/data/bss/sdata2 boundaries instead of only moving C code.
The author avoided retaining cross-file dependencies on gm_16AE.static.h by giving gmresult.c and gmresultplayer.c their own local static data declarations.
Pulling fn_80160840 and gm_801736DC into gm_1601.h acknowledged that after the split these symbols could no longer remain purely static/private in the old TU layout.
The static data relocation into gm_16F1.static.h kept large address-owned tables such as lbl_803D5648 adjacent to the functions that index them, which makes the new object easier to reason about.

The bot report shows the expected unit migration pattern: 14 functions became 100% matched in gm_16F1 and the same functions are reported as broken in gm_16AE because they no longer belong to that unit.
decomp_lessons: When splitting a monolithic TU, update splits.txt for every affected section, not just .text; this PR split .data, .bss, .sbss, and .sdata2 alongside text.
A static header that has become a catch-all can hide incorrect ownership. After a TU split, remove unrelated structs/prototypes/data from the old .static.h and create a new per-TU static header.

If another file was including a .static.h only to access one local type or BSS object, prefer moving that local declaration into the consumer file rather than preserving a broad private-header dependency.

Expect decomp.dev to report moved matches as both 'new matches' in the new unit and 'broken matches' in the old unit; read those as ownership migration rather than a true regression when the byte counts mirror each other.

Small helper extraction can be used around split work to keep nearby codegen stable or clearer, but the evidence here only shows the helpers get_unk_float and direction were introduced, not reviewer rationale.
naming_conventions: New split files follow the address-derived game-mode file naming convention: gm_16F1.c and gm_16F1.static.h for code beginning at 0x8016F180.
Unknown/internal functions retain address-derived names such as fn_8016F180, fn_8016F280, fn_8016F344, and gm_801737D8.
Known semantic names are preserved when available, such as gmDecisionGetType.
Static/global labels retain linker-address-style names such as lbl_803D5648, lbl_803D5A4C, lbl_8046DBC8, lbl_8046DBD8, and lbl_804D65A0.
The PR uses .static.h suffixes for translation-unit-private declarations/data and gm_1601.h for declarations that need to cross source-file boundaries.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Exact object-boundary split: gm_16AE text end changed from 0x80174274 to 0x8016F180 and gm_16F1 text begins at 0x8016F180.
Data-boundary split: the array/table starting at 0x803D5648 was moved to gm_16F1.static.h while gm_16AE keeps only the earlier 0x803D5620-0x803D5648 data.
BSS-boundary split: gm_16F1.static.h owns lbl_8046DBC8 and lbl_8046DBD8, matching the new bss range 0x8046DBC8-0x8046DBE8.
Public prototype fixes can be part of matching a split: fn_80160840 became externally declared and gm_80160854's return type changed to int.
Local helper get_unk_float replaces an inline/dontinline pattern around gm_8016B41C/gm_8016B558 and returns 1.0F, 1.1F, or 1.2F depending on mode values.
Local helper direction(float x) centralizes the sign-to-facing-direction mapping used by Player_SetFacingDirection.
Stack padding was adjusted from PAD_STACK(0x10) to PAD_STACK(0xC) in fn_8016E2BC after removing a local float variable.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
decomp.dev bot reported 14 new 100% matches in main/melee/gm/gm_16F1 and corresponding 14 broken matches in main/melee/gm/gm_16AE, consistent with moving those functions between units.
decomp.dev bot reported gm_80173EEC in gm_16F1 improved from 0.00% to 84.24%, while the old gm_16AE unit lost that same partial match because the function moved.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue decompiling gm_16F1 functions after gm_80173EEC reached 84.24%.
Search for later renames or type refinement of lbl_803D5A4C_t and the decision table used by gmDecisionGetType and gm_8016F208.
Check whether gm_80160854's return type change to int was reused or further refined in subsequent gm PRs.
Look for later removal of TODO comment 'this is the start of a data section' on lbl_803D5648.
Inspect later PRs that split additional gm_* translation units using the same splits.txt plus configure.py pattern.
